channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 8 hours.

item.title: 📍 Bookshelf: the untold story of a UN secretary-general | ASPI Strategist
item.description: U Thant’s rise to become UN secretary-general, the world’s top diplomat, was, to say the least, unlikely. Thant grew up in a small town in what was then rural Burma, at the time part of ...

item.title: 🏛️ As F-35 readiness lags, Pentagon seeks $13.7 billion boost: GAO | Breaking Defense
item.description: The F-35’s full mission capable rate fell to to 25 percent in FY25, the government watchdog found.

item.title: 🔐 F-22’s ‘Catfish’ 757 Testbed Spotted Carrying Raptor’s New Infrared Sensor Pod | The War Zone
item.description: The Catfish 757 has spent decades supporting the F-22 program, and that work now extends to testing external stores. The post F-22’s ‘Catfish’ 757 Testbed Spotted Carrying Raptor’s New Infrared Sensor Pod appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌍 The Iran war is a game of liar’s poker | Atlantic Council
item.description: The central question has not simply been who possesses power, but who is willing to absorb pain, tolerate risk, and continue escalating when conventional logic suggests restraint. The post The Iran war is a game of liar’s poker appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 📍 Peru’s Photo-Finish Election | Foreign Policy
item.description: The presidential race could hinge on just a few thousand votes.

item.title: 📍 Don’t wait for a disaster to develop strong US counter-drone capabilities | Atlantic Council
item.description: Security around the World Cup only underscores the need for wider efforts to ensure safety against the increasing risks from drones. The post Don’t wait for a disaster to develop strong US counter-drone capabilities appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🏛️ The Army wants to build a better data center. Can they do it? | Defense One
item.description: A call for industry ideas pulled in 200 proposals. The service is moving ahead with data centers, manufacturing upgrades, and more.

item.title: 🌍 War and Circuses | Foreign Policy
item.description: Trump juggles Iran, a World Cup, and a UFC cage fight.

item.title: 🕵️ House vote puts Section 702 on brink of lapse amid fight over acting spy chief | Defense One
item.description: Trump taps Jay Clayton as ODNI, but Democrats want a promise that Bill Pulte won't even be acting spy chief.

item.title: 🌍 Trump Backpedals on Planned Iran Strikes, Citing Deal Progress | Foreign Policy
item.description: But Iranian news reports that Tehran has not yet agreed to a deal.

item.title: 🏴 Senators want a new robot warfare-focused combatant command | Defense One
item.description: A 4-star general would lead the effort, should SASC’s version of the NDAA become law.

item.title: 🏴 Putin can no longer shield ordinary Russians from the war he unleashed | Atlantic Council
item.description: Ukraine’s recent drone strikes on St. Petersburg provided arguably the most visible indication to date that Russian President Vladimir Putin’s invasion is not going according to plan, write Maksym Beznosiuk and William Dixon. The post Putin can no longer shield ordinary Russians…

item.title: 🌍 What It Would Take To Seize Iran’s Kharg Island According To Top Former Military Leaders | The War Zone
item.description: Kharg Island, the epicenter of Iran's energy exports, is in the news again today after Trump declared he would seize it, before changing his tune. The post What It Would Take To Seize Iran’s Kharg Island According To Top Former Military Leaders appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌏 Strike, Counterstrike, Repeat | War on the Rocks
item.description: Welcome to The Adversarial. Every other week, we’ll provide you with expert analysis on America’s greatest challengers: China, Russia, Iran, North Korea, and jihadists. Read more below.***Iran Hostilities have increased notably in the last few days, effectively ending the…

item.title: 📍 Australia must know how long it can last if ships stop coming | ASPI Strategist
item.description: Australia’s prosperity runs on systems that look permanent but depend on inputs arriving from beyond its shores. While the ships keep coming, that dependence is invisible. When they stop, it becomes the only thing that ...

item.title: 🌍 Is this the end of the Iran war? | Atlantic Council
item.description: Our experts break down the latest strikes and Trump's announcement of a pending deal to end the conflict. The post Is this the end of the Iran war? appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 📍 After FCAS’s fall, Airbus and ‘Team Gen 6’ line up for new future fighter | Breaking Defense
item.description: Jean-Brice Dumont, head of airpower at Airbus Defence and Space told reporters today that despite the FCAS fighter collapse, “we have not wasted our time,” stressing that technologies linked to the defunct plane can be reused.

item.title: 🏴 Hormuz Isn’t Closed Anymore. But It’s Not Fully Open, Either. | Foreign Policy
item.description: The Trump administration is borrowing Russia’s “shadow fleet” tactics to get ships moving.

item.title: ⚛️ Saronic, Castelion to pair Marauder MUSV with Blackbeard hypersonic capability | Breaking Defense
item.description: The firms said a maritime launch demonstration is slated for 2027.

item.title: 📍 Talking Italian attack helos and skyrocketing air defense demand at the Berlin Air Show | Breaking Defense
item.description: Breaking Defense’s Tim Martin and Elisabeth Gosselin-Malo cover Day 2 of the German aerospace expo.

item.title: 🏛️ Boeing “Encouraged” By C-17 Production Restart Discussions | The War Zone
item.description: Congress recently asked the USAF for a briefing on the feasibility of buying new C-17s and allies have interest too. The post Boeing “Encouraged” By C-17 Production Restart Discussions appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌍 Trump vows to seize Iran’s Kharg Island | Defense News
item.description: Kharg Island, the linchpin of Iran’s oil industry, has once more come under focus amid a fraying ceasefire.

item.title: 📍 SASC’s $1.14T defense policy bill creates combatant command for drones | Breaking Defense
item.description: SASC members voted the bill through committee in a 18-9 vote on Wednesday.

item.title: 🌏 To stop Chinese dual-use battery dominance, the United States and South Korea need to team up | Atlantic Council
item.description: Should China establish a global monopoly across battery supply chains, the military, strategic, and commercial implications for the US-ROK alliance will be profound. The post To stop Chinese dual-use battery dominance, the United States and South Korea need to team up appeared…

item.title: ⚛️ Kongsberg Bets On High-Low Cruise Missile Mix With JSM And Rusty Dagger | The War Zone
item.description: The low-cost Rusty Dagger is being combined with the more capable and expensive Joint Strike Missile in a tiered standoff strike portfolio. The post Kongsberg Bets On High-Low Cruise Missile Mix With JSM And Rusty Dagger appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🔐 Caribbean countries are feeling the squeeze from this energy crisis—and not just from gas prices | Atlantic Council
item.description: At the 2026 Global Energy Forum, two Caribbean leaders explained that small island developing states are feeling the pinch of this crisis, in the form of high gas prices and shrinking climate adaptation finance. The post Caribbean countries are feeling the squeeze from this…

item.title: 🏴 What the US can learn from Europe on prediction markets—and vice versa | Atlantic Council
item.description: Prediction markets like Kalshi and Polymarket have surged to billions in trading volume, prompting US investigations into insider trading and manipulation. As Congress weighs new rules, Europe’s crackdown on retail binary options offers a possible—if imperfect—regulatory…

item.title: 📍 When the Beautiful Game Meets the Ugliest Ego | Foreign Policy
item.description: A recent history of the World Cup suggests the tournament can triumph over Trump.

item.title: 🏴 The Evolution of Ukraine’s Asymmetrical Combat Tactics | War on the Rocks
item.description: In 2022, Scott Sweetow wrote, “Of Roadside Bombs and Drones: Putin’s Looming Insurgency Problem,” where he argued Ukraine’s fight against Russia would rely on a combination of conventional and asymmetrical insurgent tactics. Four years of combat later, we asked Scott to revisit…

item.title: 📍 Litigation Tracker: Legal Challenges to Trump Administration Actions | Just Security
item.description: A public resource tracking all the legal challenges to the Trump administration's executive orders and actions. The post Litigation Tracker: Legal Challenges to Trump Administration Actions appeared first on Just Security.
